rulings.Case Title: MARTIN GRANADOS ET AL VS. HONEYWELL INTERNATIONAL, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 12, 2016, line 1. DEFENDANT PACIFIC GAS AND ELECTRIC COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Defendant Pacific Gas and Electric Company's motion for summary judgment is off calendar at the moving party's request. =(503/GLW)

rulings.Case Title: MARTIN GRANADOS ET AL VS. HONEYWELL INTERNATIONAL, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 12, 2016, line 1. DEFENDANT S.J. AMOROSO CONSTRUCTION CO., INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Defendant S.J. Amoroso Construction Co., Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden. Defendant failed to demonstrate that Mr. Granados did not encounter defendant's employees on any jobsites prior to the late-1970s. Even if Mr. Granados' deposition testimony can be read to conclude that he did not encounter defendant's employees prior to the late-1970s, defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that no products or materials to which defendant's employees exposed Mr. Granados contained asbestos. Plaintiffs' objections to defendant's Exhibits E, I and J (manufacturers' interrogatory responses) are sustained. Defendant failed to properly raise the issue of duty in its moving papers. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. =(503/GLW)
